title: Deno KV Database
description: Represents a Deno KV database managed by Deno Deploy. Deno KV is a key-value database built into the Deno runtime and available as a globally distributed store on Deno Deploy. Locally it uses SQLite; on Deno Deploy data is replicated across regions. Databases are accessed from Deno code via Deno.openKv() using a KV Connect URL.

databaseId.description: Unique identifier for the KV database, used in the KV Connect URL

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the database's purpose or contents

kvConnect.description: KV Connect URL used to open this database from Deno code: https://api.deno.com/databases/{database-id}/connect

organizationId.description: UUID of the organization that owns this database

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the database was created

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the database was last modified

KvKey.description: A Deno KV key, which is an ordered tuple of key parts. Parts can be strings, numbers, booleans, Uint8Arrays, or BigInts. Keys are lexicographically ordered, enabling hierarchical data modeling.

items.description: A single key part, which can be a string, number, boolean, or byte array

KvEntry.description: A key-value entry in a Deno KV database, consisting of a key tuple, a value, and a versionstamp for optimistic concurrency control

value.description: The stored value, which can be any structured-serializable JavaScript value including objects, arrays, strings, numbers, booleans, Uint8Arrays, and Deno.KvU64

versionstamp.description: A monotonically increasing hex string representing the version of this entry, used for optimistic concurrency control in atomic operations

KvListSelector.description: A selector for listing entries from a Deno KV database, supporting prefix-based and range-based queries

prefix.description: List all entries whose key starts with this prefix

start.description: List entries with keys greater than or equal to this key

end.description: List entries with keys less than this key

KvAtomicOperation.description: An atomic operation on a Deno KV database, consisting of optional checks and mutations that are applied atomically. The operation succeeds only if all checks pass.

checks.description: Preconditions that must all be satisfied for the operation to succeed. Each check verifies that a key's versionstamp matches an expected value.

mutations.description: Mutations to apply if all checks pass

KvCheck.description: A precondition check for a Deno KV atomic operation, verifying that a key's current versionstamp matches an expected value

versionstamp.description: Expected versionstamp of the entry. Null means the entry must not exist.

KvMutation.description: A single mutation to apply within a Deno KV atomic operation

type.description: The type of mutation to perform

value.description: The value to set (for set/sum/max/min mutations)

expireIn.description: Time-to-live in milliseconds for the entry (for set mutations)

KvDatabaseBackup.description: Configuration for automatic S3 backup of a Deno KV database

id.description: Unique identifier for the backup configuration

databaseId.description: UUID of the KV database being backed up

bucketName.description: Name of the S3 bucket where backups are stored

bucketRegion.description: AWS region of the S3 bucket (e.g., us-east-1)

status.description: Current status of the backup job

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the backup was configured

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the backup configuration was last modified
